## Slides: Why sequencing technology matters for pangenomics

#### The graph is only as good as the molecules and evidence used to build it

Most genomics workflows were historically built around a single reference genome. Pangenomics changes the reference model: instead of one coordinate line, we often represent a population, species, or clade as a collection of haplotypes, assemblies, and paths through a graph.

Before we ask which pangenome tool to run, we should ask what kind of molecular evidence we have. A short-read dataset, a HiFi assembly, an ultra-long nanopore dataset, and a Hi-C contact map can all describe the same biological sample, but they answer different questions.

## Slides: SNPs and small indels can be detected well by short reads

```text
Reference genome:  A C T G A C T G A C T               A C T G A C T G A C T G
                           |                                   | |
Sample read:       A C T G T C T G A C T               A C T G - - T G A C T G
                           ^                                   ^ ^
                    SNP: A → T                              small indel
```

## Slides: A 15 kb insertion absent from the reference is difficult to detect with short reads but visible in long reads

```text
Reference genome:  A -------- B

Sample genome:     A -------- [ 15 kb insertion ] -------- B

Short reads:       A ---->
                         ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?
                                                     <---- B

Long read:         A -------- [ 15 kb insertion ] -------- B
```

## Slides: Resolving the exact length of a repeat expansion requires long reads

```text
Reference: A -- CAG CAG CAG CAG -- B
                   4 repeats

Sample:    A -- CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG -- B
                              12 repeats

Short reads:        CAG CAG CAG
                CAG CAG CAG      (All reads look similar inside the repeat)
                    CAG CAG CAG    (Exact repeat number is unclear)

Long read: A -- CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG CAG -- B
```

## Slides: A heterozygous structural variant becomes interpretable only when reads are phased

```text
Maternal chromosome:  A -------- B -------- C -------- D

Paternal chromosome:  A -------- C -------- B -------- D

Unphased reads:       A -- B
                      B -- C
                      A -- C
                      C -- B
                      B -- D

Haplotype 1:          A -------- B -------- C -------- D

Haplotype 2:          A -------- C -------- B -------- D
```
